692	def _codex_job_active(session_id, detail_out=None, skipped_out=None):
693	    # (BF codex-companion parity) The codex plugin dispatches review/task work
694	    # to a DETACHED worker process that is invisible to Claude Code's
695	    # Stop-payload background_tasks[], so neither (BA) nor (TEAM-YELLOW) alone
696	    # surfaces it (terminal greens to idle while codex still runs). The
697	    # companion records each job as a JSON file with a status and the Claude
698	    # session_id (its CODEX_COMPANION_SESSION_ID is the SessionStart session_id
699	    # — the SAME id this hook receives). So an ACTIVE codex job for THIS
700	    # session is delegated agent work -> the dot stays on, like a teammate.
701	    # We do NOT inherit the codex plugin's CLAUDE_PLUGIN_DATA (it is
702	    # per-plugin), so glob the known on-disk job stores instead. "Active"
703	    # defers to the plugin's own definition (queued|running) AND requires the
704	    # job's worker pid to still be alive (_pid_alive). A transient mid-write
705	    # JSON read failure is skipped (-> a one-event missed hold that
706	    # self-corrects on the next turn-end: the SAFE direction). Bounded:
707	    # evaluated only at a real turn/subagent end (see _decide_event_type),
708	    # short-circuits on first match. Never raises.
709	    # detail_out (optional list): when provided, the holding job's
710	    # "<jobfile> pid=<pid> mtime_age=<s>s" is appended on a positive match, so
711	    # the always-on decision log can name exactly what held the dot.
712	    # skipped_out (optional list): when provided, a pid-ALIVE 'running' record
713	    # DISCARDED by the 6h staleness cap appends its
714	    # "<jobfile> pid=<pid> pid_alive=true mtime_age=<s>s cap=21600s" here. This
715	    # is the EXACT shape of the original stuck-yellow incident (a reused-pid
716	    # record aged past the cap) AND of a false-green of a genuinely long codex
717	    # job (see the cap caveat below). Without it the GREEN reason reads "no
718	    # active holds" and a reader cannot tell a stale/long codex record was
719	    # silently dropped vs there genuinely being nothing running.
720	    #
721	    # CAP CAVEAT (review findings #1/#4): the 6h cap assumes the codex-companion
722	    # worker touches its job JSON at least every <6h while alive. The producer
723	    # (the external openai-codex plugin's tracked-jobs.mjs) only rewrites the
724	    # JSON on phase/threadId/turnId CHANGE — there is NO periodic heartbeat — so
725	    # a legitimate job that stays in one phase for >6h (a long shell/model/
726	    # network wait) ages past the cap and is skipped here, dropping yellow to
727	    # green while still running. The cap is therefore a pid-REUSE heuristic, not
728	    # a liveness boundary; it deliberately self-corrects toward GREEN (the
729	    # file's documented SAFE direction) rather than risk a permanent stuck
730	    # yellow. The skipped_out logging above makes any such premature-green
731	    # immediately auditable in dot-decisions.log. A non-decaying liveness signal
732	    # (pid start-time/cmdline identity, or a companion-emitted heartbeat) would
733	    # remove the trade-off but lives in the external plugin / needs cross-platform
734	    # process introspection, so it is intentionally NOT done here.
735	    if not session_id:
736	        return False
737	    try:
738	        home = pathlib.Path.home()
739	        roots = [(home / ".claude" / "plugins" / "data", "codex*/state/*/jobs/*.json")]
740	        try:
741	            import tempfile
742	            roots.append((pathlib.Path(tempfile.gettempdir()) / "codex-companion", "*/jobs/*.json"))
743	        except Exception:
744	            pass
745	        active = ("queued", "running")
746	        for root, pattern in roots:
747	            try:
748	                for jf in root.glob(pattern):
749	                    # (R1 review) retry once on a parse failure: the worker
750	                    # rewrites the job JSON in place, so a read can land
751	                    # mid-write; skipping outright at the exact Stop moment
752	                    # would false-GREEN a still-running job.
753	                    rec = None
754	                    for _ in range(2):
755	                        try:
756	                            with open(jf, "r", encoding="utf-8") as h:
757	                                rec = json.load(h)
758	                            break
759	                        except Exception:
760	                            rec = None
761	                    if not isinstance(rec, dict):
762	                        continue
763	                    if rec.get("sessionId") != session_id:
764	                        continue
765	                    if (rec.get("status") or "") not in active:
766	                        continue
767	                    pid = rec.get("pid")
768	                    try:
769	                        int(pid)
770	                        has_pid = True
771	                    except (TypeError, ValueError):
772	                        has_pid = False
773	                    if has_pid:
774	                        # (pid-reuse guard) A 'running' record with a live pid is
775	                        # NOT trusted on its own: pids are recycled by the OS, so a
776	                        # long-dead worker's pid can read alive on an UNRELATED
777	                        # process and pin yellow forever (live incident: a 15-day-old
778	                        # 'running' record whose reused pid passed _pid_alive held the
779	                        # dot working for hours while idle). Require the job JSON to
780	                        # have been touched within 6h (the worker rewrites it as it
781	                        # progresses); an older record is stale -> skip it.
782	                        if _pid_alive(pid):
783	                            try:
784	                                import time
785	                                age = time.time() - jf.stat().st_mtime
786	                                if age < 21600:
787	                                    if detail_out is not None:
788	                                        detail_out.append(
789	                                            str(jf) + " pid=" + str(pid)
790	                                            + " mtime_age=" + str(int(age)) + "s"
791	                                        )
792	                                    return True
793	                                # pid alive but record aged past the cap: the
794	                                # exact incident (reused-pid stale record) OR a
795	                                # real >6h-in-one-phase job. Record the skip so
796	                                # the GREEN reason can name it (review #2).
797	                                if skipped_out is not None:
798	                                    skipped_out.append(
799	                                        str(jf) + " pid=" + str(pid)
800	                                        + " pid_alive=true mtime_age=" + str(int(age))
801	                                        + "s cap=21600s"
802	                                    )
803	                            except Exception:
804	                                if detail_out is not None:
805	                                    detail_out.append(str(jf) + " pid=" + str(pid) + " mtime_age=?")
806	                                return True

def _codex_job_active(session_id, detail_out=None, skipped_out=None):
    # (BF codex-companion parity) The codex plugin dispatches review/task work
    # to a DETACHED worker process that is invisible to Claude Code's
    # Stop-payload background_tasks[], so neither (BA) nor (TEAM-YELLOW) alone
    # surfaces it (terminal greens to idle while codex still runs). The
    # companion records each job as a JSON file with a status and the Claude
    # session_id (its CODEX_COMPANION_SESSION_ID is the SessionStart session_id
    # — the SAME id this hook receives). So an ACTIVE codex job for THIS
    # session is delegated agent work -> the dot stays on, like a teammate.
    # We do NOT inherit the codex plugin's CLAUDE_PLUGIN_DATA (it is
    # per-plugin), so glob the known on-disk job stores instead. "Active"
    # defers to the plugin's own definition (queued|running) AND requires the
    # job's worker pid to still be alive (_pid_alive). A transient mid-write
    # JSON read failure is skipped (-> a one-event missed hold that
    # self-corrects on the next turn-end: the SAFE direction). Bounded:
    # evaluated only at a real turn/subagent end (see _decide_event_type),
    # short-circuits on first match. Never raises.
    # detail_out (optional list): when provided, the holding job's
    # "<jobfile> pid=<pid> mtime_age=<s>s" is appended on a positive match, so
    # the always-on decision log can name exactly what held the dot.
    # skipped_out (optional list): when provided, a pid-ALIVE 'running' record
    # DISCARDED by the 6h staleness cap appends its
    # "<jobfile> pid=<pid> pid_alive=true mtime_age=<s>s cap=21600s" here. This
    # is the EXACT shape of the original stuck-yellow incident (a reused-pid
    # record aged past the cap) AND of a false-green of a genuinely long codex
    # job (see the cap caveat below). Without it the GREEN reason reads "no
    # active holds" and a reader cannot tell a stale/long codex record was
    # silently dropped vs there genuinely being nothing running.
    #
    # CAP CAVEAT (review findings #1/#4): the 6h cap assumes the codex-companion
    # worker touches its job JSON at least every <6h while alive. The producer
    # (the external openai-codex plugin's tracked-jobs.mjs) only rewrites the
    # JSON on phase/threadId/turnId CHANGE — there is NO periodic heartbeat — so
    # a legitimate job that stays in one phase for >6h (a long shell/model/
    # network wait) ages past the cap and is skipped here, dropping yellow to
    # green while still running. The cap is therefore a pid-REUSE heuristic, not
    # a liveness boundary; it deliberately self-corrects toward GREEN (the
    # file's documented SAFE direction) rather than risk a permanent stuck
    # yellow. The skipped_out logging above makes any such premature-green
    # immediately auditable in dot-decisions.log. A non-decaying liveness signal
    # (pid start-time/cmdline identity, or a companion-emitted heartbeat) would
    # remove the trade-off but lives in the external plugin / needs cross-platform
    # process introspection, so it is intentionally NOT done here.
    if not session_id:
        return False
    try:
        home = pathlib.Path.home()
        roots = [(home / ".claude" / "plugins" / "data", "codex*/state/*/jobs/*.json")]
        try:
            import tempfile
            roots.append((pathlib.Path(tempfile.gettempdir()) / "codex-companion", "*/jobs/*.json"))
        except Exception:
            pass
        active = ("queued", "running")
        for root, pattern in roots:
            try:
                for jf in root.glob(pattern):
                    # (R1 review) retry once on a parse failure: the worker
                    # rewrites the job JSON in place, so a read can land
                    # mid-write; skipping outright at the exact Stop moment
                    # would false-GREEN a still-running job.
                    rec = None
                    for _ in range(2):
                        try:
                            with open(jf, "r", encoding="utf-8") as h:
                                rec = json.load(h)
                            break
                        except Exception:
                            rec = None
                    if not isinstance(rec, dict):
                        continue
                    if rec.get("sessionId") != session_id:
                        continue
                    if (rec.get("status") or "") not in active:
                        continue
                    pid = rec.get("pid")
                    try:
                        int(pid)
                        has_pid = True
                    except (TypeError, ValueError):
                        has_pid = False
                    if has_pid:
                        # (pid-reuse guard) A 'running' record with a live pid is
                        # NOT trusted on its own: pids are recycled by the OS, so a
                        # long-dead worker's pid can read alive on an UNRELATED
                        # process and pin yellow forever (live incident: a 15-day-old
                        # 'running' record whose reused pid passed _pid_alive held the
                        # dot working for hours while idle). Require the job JSON to
                        # have been touched within 6h (the worker rewrites it as it
                        # progresses); an older record is stale -> skip it.
                        if _pid_alive(pid):
                            try:
                                import time
                                age = time.time() - jf.stat().st_mtime
                                if age < 21600:
                                    if detail_out is not None:
                                        detail_out.append(
                                            str(jf) + " pid=" + str(pid)
                                            + " mtime_age=" + str(int(age)) + "s"
                                        )
                                    return True
                                # pid alive but record aged past the cap: the
                                # exact incident (reused-pid stale record) OR a
                                # real >6h-in-one-phase job. Record the skip so
                                # the GREEN reason can name it (review #2).
                                if skipped_out is not None:
                                    skipped_out.append(
                                        str(jf) + " pid=" + str(pid)
                                        + " pid_alive=true mtime_age=" + str(int(age))
                                        + "s cap=21600s"
                                    )
                            except Exception:
                                if detail_out is not None:
                                    detail_out.append(str(jf) + " pid=" + str(pid) + " mtime_age=?")
                                return True

content.text:
Both advisors are running (Codex gpt-5.5 xhigh + a 6-agent verification workflow over watcher/renderer/host-service). Interim findings while they finish:

## Root cause — confirmed

**The stuck yellow is the BG-STALE zombie reap being event-driven only, with no time-based trigger.** Chain for the window in your screenshot (terminal `3d24ce8e…`, session `31eaea22…`):

1. Claude Code's Stop payload `background_tasks[]` **never marks idle teammates/forks as finished** — that session's "running" agent list grew 18 → 33 over the day and never shrank (known upstream behaviour; the 900s idle reap was built for exactly this after the 2026-06-24 incident).
2. At the session's **final** Stop (00:18:54 UTC), `.bgactive` (last real agent activity) was 00:04:24 — **870s old, 30s short of the 900s stale threshold** → `_bg_hold_is_stale` said "not stale" → `HOLD working` → yellow. Verified on disk: `3d24ce8e….agentbg` mtime = that exact Stop; `.bgactive` untouched since.
3. The reap runs **only inside hook handlers** (`Stop` / `SubagentStop` / manual-compact `SessionStart`) in `pane-map-hook.ts:1340` / `:1281` / `:1233`. No prompt since → no hook event → nothing ever re-evaluates → yellow latched indefinitely. It self-heals only when you next type a prompt (next Stop sees >900s idle → green).

Terminal `1222248b` yesterday proves the mechanism works when an event *does* arrive: `GREEN: bg-agents idle >900s, stale-reaped (Stop)` at 01:18.

## Recurrence — matches "keeps happening to other windows"

Log history: **9 terminals** ended their logged life stuck in a bg-tasks HOLD (Jun 22 → today), and 91 episodes where a HOLD sat >30 min before any next event arrived.

## Fix direction (pending advisor input)

A periodic sweep in the always-running Electron main process: scan `~/.superset/agent-subagent-running/*.agentbg`; where `.bgactive` is >900s stale, synthesize the green/blue turn-end — skipping terminals with live `askq` red-guard owners, live subagent run-dir markers, or an active codex-companion job. Key open question the advisors are validating: whether to host it in the JSONL watcher's existing poll loop and how to reuse notify.py's decision logic without a second drifting implementation.

Will report the consolidated fix design when Codex + the workflow return.

file.content:
# name so a marker write can never fail on an unexpected path character.
    sub_agent_id = (payload.get("agent_id") or payload.get("agentId") or "").strip()
    sub_agent_id = "".join(c for c in sub_agent_id if c.isalnum() or c in "-_")
    # (BA) Cloud/background-session signal. The Stop (and SubagentStop) hook
    # payload carries background_tasks[] (Claude Code >= 2.1.145); a NON-EMPTY
    # array means background work is still running after the turn ended.
    # (TEAM-YELLOW) the typed entries split into agent work (yellow) vs
    # shell-only (blue) — see _split_background. Absent on older versions ->
    # both falsy -> behaves exactly as before. session_crons (scheduled
    # wakeups) are intentionally NOT counted (pending, not running).
    # None-check (not "or"): an EMPTY list is an authoritative "nothing is
    # running" and must reach _running_bg_ids, where "or" would collapse it
    # to the other key's None. _split_background treats [] and None the same.
    bg_tasks = payload.get("background_tasks")
    if bg_tasks is None:
        bg_tasks = payload.get("backgroundTasks")
    has_background, has_agent_background = _split_background(bg_tasks)
    # (MARKER-RECONCILE) only a payload that actually carries the list shape
    # is authoritative; absent/odd-shaped -> None -> no reconciliation (an
    # older Claude Code without background_tasks behaves exactly as before).
    bg_ids = _running_bg_ids(bg_tasks) if isinstance(bg_tasks, (list, dict)) else None
    # (COMPACT-YELLOW) PreCompact carries trigger ("manual"|"auto");
    # SessionStart carries source ("startup"|"resume"|"clear"|"compact").
    trigger = str(payload.get("trigger") or "").strip()
    source = str(payload.get("source") or "").strip()

    url = os.environ.get("SUPERSET_HOST_AGENT_HOOK_URL", "").strip()
    terminal_id = os.environ.get("SUPERSET_TERMINAL_ID", "").strip()
    agent_id = (os.environ.get("SUPERSET_AGENT_ID") or "claude").strip()

    # (BA diagnostic) When background_tasks is non-empty, dump its shape so we can
    # tell an actively-working teammate/subagent (should be YELLOW) apart from a
    # passive background shell/cloud session (BLUE). Truncated; never raises.
    if has_background:
        try:
            _log({
                "event": event,
                "terminalId": terminal_id,
                "sessionId": session_id,
                "action": "bg-tasks-debug",
                "bgCount": len(bg_tasks) if isinstance(bg_tasks, list) else -1,
                "bgTasks": json.dumps(bg_tasks)[:1500],
            })
        except Exception:
            pass

    if not terminal_id:
        _log({
            "event": event, "tool": tool, "mappedEventType": None,
            "terminalId": terminal_id, "sessionId": session_id, "url": url,
            "action": "skip-no-terminal",
        })
        return

    # (PANE-MAP-UNSTEAL) the ending session's pane mapping must not outlive it
    # on this terminal (see _drop_pane_map_if_ours).
    if event == "SessionEnd":
        _drop_pane_map_if_ours(session_id, terminal_id)

    # Also performs the SubagentStart/Stop marker side-effects, so the
    # yellow-hold state stays consistent even when url is momentarily absent.
    reason_out = []
    event_type = _decide_event_type(
        event,
        tool,
        terminal_id,
        sub_agent_id,
        has_background,
        has_agent_background,
        bg_ids,

input.command:
f="/c/Users/khair/.claude/projects/C--Users-khair--superset-worktrees-7ce6c168-a8cb-46d6-a11c-80d91c98e4a7-delivery-mode-for-monthlies/31eaea22-4a0c-47c3-a016-257c2f66cae6.jsonl"; python - "$f" <<'EOF'
import sys, json, re
f = sys.argv[1]
spawns = {}     # teammate name -> last spawn ts
idles = {}      # teammate name -> last idle_notification ts
msgs = {}       # teammate name -> last agent-message ts
sends = {}      # name -> last SendMessage TO it (re-wake)
last_ts = None
for ln in open(f, encoding="utf-8", errors="replace"):
    try: o = json.loads(ln)
    except: continue
    ts = o.get("timestamp")
    if ts: last_ts = ts
    s = json.dumps(o)
    m = re.search(r'<teammate-message teammate_id=\\"([^\\"]+)\\"', s)
    if m:
        name = m.group(1)
        if "idle_notification" in s: idles[name] = ts
        else: msgs[name] = ts
    m2 = re.search(r'<agent-message from=\\"([^\\"]+)\\"', s)
    if m2: msgs[m2.group(1)] = ts
    # Agent tool spawns (teammate creation) + SendMessage tool calls
    if o.get("type") == "assistant":
        try:
            for c in o["message"]["content"]:
                if c.get("type") == "tool_use":
                    if c["name"] == "Agent":
                        nm = (c["input"] or {}).get("name")
                        if nm: spawns[nm] = ts
                    if c["name"] == "SendMessage":
                        to = (c["input"] or {}).get("to")
                        if to: sends[to] = ts
        except Exception: pass
print("file last ts:", last_ts)
names = sorted(set(spawns) | set(idles) | set(msgs) | set(sends) - {"team-lead"})
print(f"{'name':38} {'spawned':22} {'last-idle':22} {'last-msg':22} {'last-sent-to':22}")
for n in names:
    print(f"{n:38} {str(spawns.get(n)):22} {str(idles.get(n)):22} {str(msgs.get(n)):22} {str(sends.get(n)):22}")
print("\ncounts: spawns", len(spawns), "idles", len(idles))
EOF

content.content:
ng compact `SessionStart` arrives.
- Renderer `sessionStorage` persists full `sources`, including `working` axes, at `store.ts:556-564`; a Ctrl+R/error-boundary reload can rehydrate stale yellow with no new event.

I do not see the `.bgactive` seeding rule regranting freshness every turn-end: it only touches when missing at `pane-map-hook.ts:1185-1186`, and stale branches deliberately leave the old mtime in place.

**Recommended Fix**

Put the canonical sweep in host-service, not renderer and not only Electron main. Host-service owns `terminalId -> workspaceId`, event bus broadcast, and `terminalAgentStore` updates: see `packages\host-service\src\trpc\router\notifications\notifications.ts:54-95`, `packages\host-service\src\events\event-bus.ts:165-169`, and `packages\host-service\src\terminal-agents\store.ts:63-113`.

Concrete design:

1. Factor `notifications.hook` into an internal helper, e.g. `recordAgentLifecycle(...)`, that maps event type, resolves `terminal_sessions.originWorkspaceId`, broadcasts, and records `terminalAgentStore`.
2. Start a `setInterval` sweep from `packages\host-service\src\app.ts` after `eventBus` and `terminalAgentStore` are created around lines 138-155.
3. Sweep `~\.superset\agent-subagent-running\*.agentbg`; if matching `.bgactive` is older than 900s, no `.askq` owners exist, terminal is still active, and the host binding last event proves the turn already ended, clear the hold.
4. Do not emit plain `Stop` unless you can prove no red is active. More robust: add a quiet stale-reap lifecycle event such as `AgentBackgroundStale` that clears `working` but never clears `permission`. Add a `BackgroundRunningStale` variant only if you need to preserve blue.
5. Renderer handling in `statusTransitions.ts` should clear `working`, preserve `permission`, and set `review` only when the target is not visible. Suppress chimes in `lifecycleEvents.ts`, like `SubagentActive` and `BackgroundRunning`.

**Edge Cases To Cover**

- New user prompt starts after the stale hold but before the sweep: skip if host binding last event is `Start`/`PermissionRequest`.
- `.askq` has any owner: skip or use a red-preserving event only.
- `.bgactive` refreshed between scan and emit: re-stat immediately before clearing.
- Active codex companion job for the same session: keep yellow; add a codex-hold marker if needed so codex-only holds can be swept later.
- Renderer missed the sweep event during reload: add a rehydrate/reconnect reconciliation so persisted `working` cannot outlive host binding state.
- Terminal exited/disposed: do not emit agent lifecycle; clean markers best-effort.
- If `.bgactive` is missing, seed it once and skip, matching the current benefit-of-doubt behavior.

Simpler acceptable patch: add a main-process watcher sweep that emits through host-service `notifications.hook`. It would fix this desktop symptom faster, but it is less robust because Electron `notificationsEmitter` is not the source of truth and can miss host binding state.

questions.question: Implement the stuck-yellow fix? L1 = deterministic teammate-idle tracking from lead transcript (instant correct green; catches the big swarm cases like your stuck window). L3 = timed sweep enforcing the existing 900s idle rule (catches teammates that die silently with zero log trace — proven to exist in session 68bced18 — plus the Ctrl+R stale-rehydrate case). L2 (900s rule) already exists.

options.label: L1 + L3 (Recommended)
options.description: Full coverage: instant deterministic green for reporting teammates + 15-min-max backstop for silent deaths. Both Codex and my analysis agree neither alone covers all observed cases.

options.label: L1 only
options.description: Pure event-driven, no timers. Your stuck window's case fixed instantly. BUT silently-dead teammates (2 observed in one session) still pin yellow until you next prompt that window.

options.label: L3 only
options.description: Timer backstop only. All stuck-yellows clear within ~15 min of going idle, but the common swarm case waits that full window instead of greening instantly.

questions.question: Where should the L3 sweep live? Both converge to the same renderer+host state (renderer forwards every lifecycle event to host-service).

options.label: Watcher (Recommended)
options.description: Electron main, agent-jsonl-watcher poll loop. Smallest blast radius; reuses the existing API-ABORT-RELEASE synthesis template with askq-red + codex-job guards already implemented there.

options.label: Host-service
options.description: Codex's canonical pick: host owns terminal→workspace bindings + terminalAgentStore, so the sweep can consult binding last-event as an extra guard. Bigger change, touches packages/host-service.

const NOTIFY_SCRIPT = `#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""Superset Claude agent-status notify hook.

Installed by agent-jsonl-watcher/pane-map-hook.ts. POSTs each Claude lifecycle
event to the host-service (SUPERSET_HOST_AGENT_HOOK_URL) so the agent-status
dots are driven event-driven — reviving what the dead bash notify.sh did.

Event -> eventType mapping (NEVER defaults to Stop on an unknown event; an
unmapped event is a silent no-op, exactly like notify.sh):
  UserPromptSubmit            -> Start            (working / yellow)
  Stop                        -> Stop             (review/green) UNLESS a
                                 subagent is still running (then suppressed —
                                 stay yellow; greens on the last SubagentStop).
                                 With background_tasks[] still running, the
                                 entry TYPES decide: any agent-type entry
                                 (subagent/teammate/workflow) -> SubagentActive
                                 (yellow; red-respecting in the renderer);
                                 shell-only -> BackgroundRunning (blue)
  SessionEnd                  -> Stop             (review / green; clears state)
  StopFailure(main loop)      -> Stop + clears state, UNLESS a codex-companion
                                 job for this session is still alive ->
                                 SubagentActive (BF: codex runs on its OWN API;
                                 a Claude rate-limit abort does not stop it)
  StopFailure(in a subagent)  -> (STOPFAIL-SUBAGENT) self-scoped: drops only that
                                 fork's markers, never the shared session state /
                                 the main question guard; the central red guard
                                 keeps a still-pending AskUserQuestion red
  Notification                -> PermissionRequest (permission / red)
  PreToolUse(AskUserQuestion) -> PermissionRequest (red)   else no-op
  PostToolUse(main loop)      -> Start             (working — clears red after
                                 a permission approval or an answered question:
                                 the main loop is sequential, so a completed
                                 main-loop tool proves the red was handled)
  PostToolUse(in a subagent)  -> SubagentActive    ((SUBTOOL-RED) payload carries
                                 agent_id ONLY for tool calls inside a subagent;

Server maps Start->working, Stop->review, PermissionRequest->permission and
returns {"result":{"data":{"json":{"success":true,...}}}}. Uses only stdlib
urllib so it has no third-party dependency. Silent on every failure path — a
broken hook must NEVER raise or abort the agent.
"""
import datetime
import json
import os
import pathlib
import sys
import urllib.request


def _log(record):
    # Append one JSON line to ~/.superset/agent-notify-hook.log. Never raises.
    if os.environ.get("SUPERSET_AGENT_WATCHER_DEBUG") == "0":
        return
    try:
        record["ts"] = datetime.datetime.now().isoformat()
        log_dir = pathlib.Path.home() / ".superset"
        log_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
        with open(log_dir / "agent-notify-hook.log", "a", encoding="utf-8") as h:
            h.write(json.dumps(record) + "\\n")
    except Exception:
        pass


def _decision_log(terminal_id, session_id, event_type, reason):
    # ALWAYS-ON (independent of SUPERSET_AGENT_WATCHER_DEBUG) one-line audit of a
    # terminal turn-end dot decision, so a stuck dot can be diagnosed after the
    # fact without reproducing it. Written ONLY at terminal decisions (Stop /
    # SubagentStop / StopFailure / manual-compact finish) — NOT on every
    # PostToolUse — so it stays cheap and bounded. Rotates at ~1MB (single
    # .log.1 backup). Best-effort: ANY failure here is swallowed so the hook
    # still POSTs even if logging breaks. Never raises.
    try:
        ts = datetime.datetime.now(datetime.timezone.utc).isoformat()
        log_dir = pathlib.Path.home() / ".superset" / "logs"
        log_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
        log_path = log_dir / "dot-decisions.log"
        try:
            if log_path.stat().st_size > 1048576:
                backup = log_dir / "dot-decisions.log.1"
                try:
                    if backup.exists():
                        backup.unlink()
                except Exception:
                    pass
                log_path.rename(backup)
        except Exception:
            pass
        line = (
            ts
            + " terminal=" + str(terminal_id)
            + " session=" + str(session_id)
            + " eventType=" + str(event_type)
            + " " + str(reason)
        )
        with open(log_path, "a", encoding="utf-8") as h:
            h.write(line + "\\n")
    except Exception:
        pass

def _compact_marker_path(terminal_id):
    # (COMPACT-YELLOW) records "a compaction is running" plus its trigger
    # (manual|auto) so the finish path knows how to clear the dot.
    return (
        pathlib.Path.home()
        / ".superset"
        / "agent-subagent-running"
        / (terminal_id + ".compacting")
    )


def _agentbg_marker_path(terminal_id):
    # (TEAM-YELLOW) records "the latest Stop/SubagentStop snapshot saw
    # agent-type background work still running". Consumed by the manual-compact
    # finish path, whose SessionStart payload carries NO background_tasks of
    # its own — without it, /compact ending while teammates/workflows run
    # would false-green. Refreshed from every turn-end payload; a stale marker
    # errs yellow (safe) and clears at the next bg-free turn end.
    return (
        pathlib.Path.home()
        / ".superset"
        / "agent-subagent-running"
        / (terminal_id + ".agentbg")
    )


def _shellbg_marker_path(terminal_id):
    # Sibling of .agentbg for the BLUE direction: records "the latest
    # Stop/SubagentStop snapshot saw ONLY shell-type background work still
    # running". Consumed by the same manual-compact finish path so /compact
    # ending while a background shell runs restores the BackgroundRunning
    # blue instead of false-greening (verified live 2026-06-11). A stale
    # marker errs blue-lingers — the same accepted (BA)/(BE) tradeoff — and
    # clears at the next turn end.
    return (
        pathlib.Path.home()
        / ".superset"
        / "agent-subagent-running"
        / (terminal_id + ".shellbg")
    )


def _bgactive_marker_path(terminal_id):
    # (BG-STALE) Last time REAL teammate/subagent activity was seen for this
    # terminal (a SubagentStart or a subagent-scoped PostToolUse). Consulted at a
    # turn-end agent-hold: an agent set Claude Code still reports "running" but
    # that has produced no activity for _BG_STALE_SECONDS is idle/zombie and must
    # stop pinning the lead's dot yellow (the harness never flips an idle
    # long-lived teammate, or one that died without a clean SubagentStop, to a
    # finished status). Refreshed ONLY on genuine forward activity (never on the
    # hold itself, which would keep it perpetually fresh); the JSONL watcher
    # re-asserts yellow within its poll if a reaped set turns out to still write.
    return (
        pathlib.Path.home()
        / ".superset"
        / "agent-subagent-running"
        / (terminal_id + ".bgactive")
    )

def _teamstate_path(terminal_id):
    # (TEAMMATE-IDLE) incremental parse cache for the lead transcript's
    # teammate ledger: {"path": <transcript>, "offset": <bytes consumed>,
    # "state": {name: "active"|"idle"}}. Keyed by transcript path, so a
    # /resume (new transcript file) or terminalId reuse self-invalidates into
    # a full reparse. Corruption tolerated: any read failure falls back to
    # offset 0 — self-healing, never trusted blindly.
    return (
        pathlib.Path.home()
        / ".superset"
        / "agent-subagent-running"
        / (terminal_id + ".teamstate.json")
    )

content.content:
24:| Team/workflow work shows working | background_tasks[] entry types split the post-turn dot: agent-type work (teammate/subagent/workflow) holds yellow; shell-only goes blue | `(TEAM-YELLOW)` |
43:| Untagged background-agent tool completions never stomp the red | teammate/fork tool completions (ordinary Read/Bash/Edit/…) stream in attributed to the PARENT session WITHOUT an agent_id, so the SUBTOOL-RED/ASYNC-TOOL-RED checks miss them and they would Start-clear a pending AskUserQuestion red. A `<terminalId>.askq/` DIR holds one marker PER OWNER (the raising agent_id, or `_main`), set synchronously at PreToolUse:AskUserQuestion; while ANY marker exists an untagged ordinary PostToolUse maps to the red-respecting SubagentActive (and refreshes `.bgactive`, since the blocked main loop means that tool is genuine teammate activity). Per-owner so concurrent questions (main + a subagent) are independent. The renderer has ONE permission axis per terminal, so a single CENTRAL GUARD wraps the whole decision and enforces "rendered red ⇔ `.askq` dir non-empty" in one place: it counts owners before and after the inner decision, then (DOWNGRADE) turns any red-CLEARING result (Start, or a green/blue turn-end) into the red-respecting SubagentActive while any owner remains (stamping `.mainstopped` on a held turn-end so the last SubagentStop still finalizes green), and (UPGRADE) turns a NON-clearing result (SubagentActive yellow/agent/codex hold, or a None no-op) into Start when this event removed the LAST owner — so a just-answered/cancelled/reaped question's red can't latch behind a yellow hold. One choke point, so NO branch — answer, UserPromptSubmit, Stop, SubagentStop (incl. its cancelled-with-question and still-other-work paths), the compact paths — can drop a co-pending red OR strand a resolved one. (The native-notification manager likewise only chimes "complete" on Stop and "awaiting" on permission, never on SubagentActive.) An owner's marker is cleared by its own answer (PostToolUse:AskUserQuestion), its SubagentStop, or the main turn boundary (Stop/UserPromptSubmit clear only `_main`); dead non-`_main` owners are reaped against `background_tasks` ids on Stop/SubagentStop (MARKER-RECONCILE-style) so a subagent that died without a clean stop can't pin red; the whole dir clears on session end / API abort / fresh SessionStart (its hook is registered UNSCOPED so the non-compact source is reached), and a main interrupt clears only `_main` (detached teammates survive). The JSONL watcher emits its direct interrupt/abort `Stop` as `SubagentActive` instead while a live `.askq` owner remains (it bypasses the Python guard). Synchronous + per-owner = no timing window and no cross-question clearing; a tool-PERMISSION red (no `.askq` marker) still clears on its approved tool's main-loop Start — no approval-clear regression. SubagentStop of a subagent that stopped WITH its own question still open emits Start (central guard keeps any other owner) so a cancelled subagent question's red can't latch; the watcher's held interrupt also stamps `.mainstopped` so a later SubagentStop still finalizes green; and an askq owner is NEVER reaped from run-marker age alone (a subagent legitimately BLOCKED on an open question has no activity, so age-reaping it would drop a LIVE red) — when `background_tasks` is absent a leaked owner just errs YELLOW until a session/abort/fresh-start boundary clears it. Accepted limits (all inherent to the fire-and-forget hook→POST→last-writer-wins pipeline, not specific to this feature): a generic Notification (tool-permission) red is unguarded (its approval is indistinguishable from a teammate tool without a request id); a sub-ms TOCTOU window (a Start in flight before a question opens); and a stale watcher transcript chunk carrying an old API-abort can clear a question raised during the lag (genuine aborts kill the shared-API subagents, so this is only the narrow abort→recover→new-question-within-one-poll window). Fully closing the last two would need pipeline-wide event sequencing | `(UNTAGGED-BG-RED)` |
47:| Leaked yellow-hold markers self-heal | a SubagentStop arriving with a mismatched/missing agent_id leaks its run-dir marker and pins the dot yellow with nothing running; at every Stop/SubagentStop the payload's background_tasks[] (ground truth) reaps any marker not listed as still running | `(MARKER-RECONCILE)` |
56:| Idle/zombie teammate hold reaped | an agent set Claude Code still reports `running` in background_tasks[] but that has produced NO activity (SubagentStart / subagent-scoped PostToolUse refresh a `.bgactive` timer) for 15 min stops pinning the lead's dot yellow — the harness never flips an idle long-lived teammate (or one that died without a clean SubagentStop) to a finished status, so the hold previously lasted until the team was disbanded / the session ended. The JSONL watcher re-asserts yellow if a reaped set is in fact still writing | `(BG-STALE)` |
72:(TEAM-YELLOW)	apps/desktop/src/main
99:(MARKER-RECONCILE)	apps/desktop/src/main
109:(BG-STALE)	apps/desktop/src/main

content.content:
50	| Dot state survives renderer reloads | the v2-notifications dot store persists to sessionStorage — an in-place window reload (Ctrl+R / error boundary / crash recovery) no longer wipes every dot; the background-running blue has no self-heal until the next turn end, so a reload used to hide a running background task for hours. Clears on real app restart (no stale dots across launches) | `(DOT-PERSIST)` |
51	| Agent hook fork-diet | every agent lifecycle hook parses + escapes JSON with bash builtins (read, `[[ =~ ]]`, parameter expansion); the old grep/tr + sed pipelines forked ~30 subprocesses per call and crashed the x64-emulated msys2 runtime on Windows ARM64 (the add_item errno-1 fork cascade that wedged every chat's hooks). Wire payload unchanged | `(HOOK-FORK-DIET)` |
52	| Master cards archive-only | a repo's master / non-git master card (`type === "main"`) can never be hard-removed from the sidebar — "Remove from Sidebar" archives it (recoverable under the project's Archived section) instead of hiding it into oblivion. `archiveWorkspace` inserts an archived row for an auto-included main that has none yet | `(MASTER-ARCHIVE-ONLY)` |
53	| Recycle Bin (soft delete) | the default "Delete" for a worktree/branch workspace (sidebar + kanban, incl. unbound Queued cards) is now a lossless, recoverable move-to-bin (silent — no dialog/toast; worktree + branch + terminals untouched, exactly like Archive). Restore always returns to ACTIVE; nothing is ever auto-purged. Permanent git destroy is relocated to "Delete permanently" inside the bin (the old destroy dialog) + a one-confirm "Empty Recycle Bin". A renderer-only retention setting (default 30 days) is a DISPLAY filter only — the bin shows items deleted within the last N days, older ones collapse behind a per-bin "Show all" toggle with an "N hidden by filter" footer. Per-project sidebar "Recycle Bin (N)" section + per-column kanban sub-section, sorted by `deletedAt` DESC. Mains stay archive-only (never deletable) | `(RECYCLE-BIN)` |
54	| Auto-resume stuck chats | when a Claude chat dies on an API failure the JSONL watcher classifies it (deny-list-first: rate-limit-with-reset-time / transient-rate-limit / server-error / connection-drop / half-stop are resumable; auth/policy/bad-image/model-unavailable are notify-only) after a finality/quiescence gate, and a durable MAIN-side registry auto-sends "resume from exactly where everything was left" — fire-at-parsed-reset-time (+30s, DST-correct, fire-now-if-already-past, ≤8d sane cap) for usage limits, else a 60s→×3 backoff capped at 5 sends / 24h. Every send is gated at fire time (toggle on, still the last meaningful transcript line, host-service `commandRunning`=idle + agent-session binding match) so it can never auto-confirm a menu, land mid-turn, or run at a bare shell. Any trusted interaction with that terminal cancels (persisted tombstone survives reload/restart). Account-wide cohorts are jittered + concurrency-capped. Global toggle (default on). Claude-only in v1 — Codex auto-resume is deferred (the on-disk corpus has no validated standalone usage-limit signal). Dots untouched (separate IPC variant + toasts) | `(AUTO-RESUME)`, `autoResumeManager`, `writeInputIfIdleSession`, `useAutoResumeActivity`, `AutoResumeController` |
55	| Dot-decision log + codex pid-reuse cap | superset-notify.py writes an always-on bounded `~/.superset/logs/dot-decisions.log` line at each terminal turn-end decision (the POSTed eventType + WHY a hold won — codex job / background_tasks / subagent markers / GREEN / BLUE / RED), and `_codex_job_active` age-caps pid-bearing `running` codex job records at 6h so a recycled PID can't pin the dot yellow forever (a cap-skipped stale record is logged, so a premature-green is auditable) | `_decision_log` |
56	| Idle/zombie teammate hold reaped | an agent set Claude Code still reports `running` in background_tasks[] but that has produced NO activity (SubagentStart / subagent-scoped PostToolUse refresh a `.bgactive` timer) for 15 min stops pinning the lead's dot yellow — the harness never flips an idle long-lived teammate (or one that died without a clean SubagentStop) to a finished status, so the hold previously lasted until the team was disbanded / the session ended. The JSONL watcher re-asserts yellow if a reaped set is in fact still writing | `(BG-STALE)` |
57	| Nightly dependency-consistency + lock regen | the nightly merge can introduce dependency-state drift that only the build discovers (after a windows-arm64 runner spins up): a stale fork root `overrides` entry that pins a package to a version different from the merged workspace pin (electron-builder "production dependency not found …"), or a `bun.lock` left stale by an upstream package.json bump (frozen-install fails). The merge now runs an override-consistency gate (fails loud on any exact override↔pin mismatch) + regenerates `bun.lock` to match the merged manifests with the build's exact bun version (which also fails loud, in the cheap merge job, if the merged manifests don't resolve or a pin is age-blocked) — so this class of build failure is caught/fixed before the windows-arm64 build spins up, baseline untouched on failure (recovery loop). No full `--frozen-lockfile` install runs here: the merge runner is linux, so a platform-specific install quirk would be a false abort, and the regenerated lock is consistent with the manifests by construction. The fork carries NO mastra `overrides` and pins `@mastra/core`/`mastracode` to upstream's exact versions in all three workspaces (upstream keeps mastra consistent via identical exact pins; the seed commit's redundant override + a recovery merge that resolved the pins DOWN to 0.18.0 were the recurring breakage, both corrected) | `(LOCK-REGEN)` |
58

input.new_string:
| Idle/zombie teammate hold reaped | an agent set Claude Code still reports `running` in background_tasks[] but that has produced NO activity (SubagentStart / subagent-scoped PostToolUse refresh a `.bgactive` timer) for 15 min stops pinning the lead's dot yellow — the harness never flips an idle long-lived teammate (or one that died without a clean SubagentStop) to a finished status, so the hold previously lasted until the team was disbanded / the session ended. The JSONL watcher re-asserts yellow if a reaped set is in fact still writing | `(BG-STALE)` |
| Idle teammates green deterministically | Claude Code reports finished teammates in the Stop payload's background_tasks[] as `status:"running"` FOREVER (captured live: 33 teammate entries, all "running", for a session whose 44 teammates had all finished hours earlier) — and the (BG-STALE) reap only runs inside hook events, so a session whose LAST event landed while the set was <15 min idle latched yellow indefinitely (nothing ever re-evaluated; 9 terminals observed stuck). superset-notify.py now keeps a per-terminal teammate LEDGER parsed incrementally from the lead session transcript (payload `transcript_path`; offset+state cached in `<terminalId>.teamstate.json`, keyed by transcript path so /resume self-invalidates): Agent-spawn / SendMessage-wake / non-idle teammate- or agent-messages mark a name active, an `idle_notification` teammate-message marks it idle. At Stop/SubagentStop, when EVERY tracked teammate is provably idle, unfinished teammate-type entries are dropped BEFORE the split/snapshot/hold decision — the lead greens (or blues on a shell remainder) at its actual final turn end instead of holding forever. Positive proof only: no transcript / no teammates seen / any parse error / any teammate not idle → entries kept (old behaviour + (BG-STALE) unchanged behind it); raw fork agentIds ("a"+hex) are excluded from the predicate (forks have real hook lifecycles and would never idle-notify); subagent/workflow/shell entries always pass through untouched; bg_ids marker reconciliation still uses the RAW list. Decision log carries a `[teammate-idle: dropped N ...]` suffix for auditability | `(TEAMMATE-IDLE)` |

cd "/c/Users/khair/Code Projects/fork-superset-windows-arm64" && cat > tmp/commit-msg.txt <<'EOF'
fix(desktop): deterministic teammate-idle green for the agent status dot (TEAMMATE-IDLE)

Claude Code reports finished teammates in the Stop payload's
background_tasks[] as status "running" forever, and the (BG-STALE) 900s
idle reap only runs inside hook events - so a session whose LAST event
landed while the zombie set was <15min idle latched YELLOW indefinitely
(live case: final Stop at 870s idle, stuck for hours; 9 terminals
observed stuck historically).

superset-notify.py now keeps an incremental per-terminal teammate ledger
parsed from the lead session transcript (payload transcript_path; offset
+ state cached in <terminalId>.teamstate.json, keyed by transcript path
so /resume self-invalidates): Agent-spawn / SendMessage-wake / non-idle
teammate- or agent-messages mark a name active, an idle_notification
teammate-message marks it idle. At Stop/SubagentStop, when every tracked
teammate is provably idle, unfinished teammate-type entries are dropped
BEFORE the split / agentbg snapshot / hold decision, so the lead greens
(or blues on a shell remainder) at its real final turn end.

Positive proof only: no transcript / no teammates / parse error / any
teammate not idle -> entries kept (old behaviour, (BG-STALE) unchanged
behind it). Raw fork agentIds ("a"+hex) are excluded from the predicate;
subagent/workflow/shell entries always pass through; bg_ids marker
reconciliation still uses the RAW list; decision log gains a
"[teammate-idle: dropped N ...]" suffix; ledger cache cleared on
SessionEnd. String-scanning parser (no regex) to avoid TS template
literal backslash swallowing.

Replay-validated against the live stuck session (44 teammates, 33 zombie
entries -> GREEN with drop note) plus guard matrix: never-idle teammates
HOLD, missing transcript HOLD, workflow entry still HOLDs after teammate
drop, shell remainder BLUE, warm-cache idempotent.
EOF
git commit -F tmp/commit-msg.txt && git log --oneline -1

content.text:
`low effort → 1 diff pass → no verify → ≤4 findings`

## Turn 1 — read

One tool call: read the unified diff (`git diff @{upstream}...HEAD; git diff HEAD`
to cover both committed and uncommitted changes, or `git diff main...HEAD` /
the target passed as an argument). Skip test/fixture
hunks (`test/`, `spec/`, `__tests__/`, `*_test.*`, `*.test.*`,
`fixtures/`, `testdata/`) — test-file changes are not reviewed at this level.
No subagents, no full-file reads.

## Turn 2 — findings

Flag runtime-correctness bugs visible from the hunk alone: inverted/wrong
condition, off-by-one, null/undefined deref where adjacent lines show the value
can be absent, removed guard, falsy-zero check, missing `await`,
wrong-variable copy-paste, error swallowed in a catch that should propagate.
Also flag — still from the hunk alone — new code that duplicates an existing
helper visible in the diff context, and dead code the diff leaves behind.

Do **not** flag style, naming, perf, missing tests, or anything outside the
hunk.

Output at most **4 findings**, most-severe first, one line each:
`path/to/file.ext:123 — what's wrong and the concrete failure`. If nothing
qualifies, output exactly `(none)`.

+def _teamstate_path(terminal_id):
+    # (TEAMMATE-IDLE) incremental parse cache for the lead transcript's
+    # teammate ledger: {"path": <transcript>, "offset": <bytes consumed>,
+    # "state": {name: "active"|"idle"}}. Keyed by transcript path, so a
+    # /resume (new transcript file) or terminalId reuse self-invalidates into
+    # a full reparse. Corruption tolerated: any read failure falls back to
+    # offset 0 — self-healing, never trusted blindly.
+    return (
+        pathlib.Path.home()
+        / ".superset"
+        / "agent-subagent-running"
+        / (terminal_id + ".teamstate.json")
+    )
+
+
 def _askq_dir(terminal_id):

@@ -885,6 +900,212 @@ def _split_background(bg_tasks):
     return (has_any, has_agent)
 
 
+# (TEAMMATE-IDLE) Claude Code NEVER flips an idle teammate's background_tasks[]
+# entry off "running" (captured live 2026-07-10: 33 teammate entries, all
+# status "running", for a session whose 44 teammates had ALL finished hours
+# earlier), so the payload alone cannot green a lead whose teammates are done —
+# once no further hook event arrived, the dot stayed yellow forever. The lead
+# TRANSCRIPT has the missing truth: every teammate that finishes delivers an
+# idle_notification teammate-message into it, and every (re)activation is
+# visible too (an Agent spawn tool_use, a SendMessage tool_use to it, a
+# non-idle teammate-message/agent-message from it). Track that ledger
+# incrementally and, at a turn end, DROP unfinished teammate-type entries when
+# every tracked teammate's last event is an idle_notification. POSITIVE proof
+# only: no transcript, no teammates seen, a parse error, or ANY teammate not
+# provably idle -> keep the entries (the safe yellow direction — exactly the
+# old behaviour, with the (BG-STALE) idle reap still behind it). A teammate
+# that dies WITHOUT ever reporting stays "active" in the ledger and keeps the
+# hold: from the logs that case is indistinguishable from a long think, so it
+# is deliberately left to (BG-STALE).
+
+
+def _team_scan_text(state, text):
+    # Update the name -> "active"|"idle" ledger from one transcript text blob.
+    # String scanning only (NO regex): this file is a TS template literal, and
+    # single backslashes in regex escapes would be silently swallowed.
+    i = 0
+    tag = '<teammate-message teammate_id="'
+    while True:
+        i = text.find(tag, i)
+        if i < 0:
+            break
+        j = i + len(tag)
+        k = text.find('"', j)
+        if k < 0:
+            break
+        name = text[j:k]
+        end = text.find("</teammate-message>", k)
+        body = text[k:end] if end > 0 else text[k:]
+        if name:
+            state[name] = "idle" if '"idle_notification"' in body else "active"
+        i = k
+    a = 0
+    tag2 = '<agent-message from="'
+    while True:
+        a = text.find(tag2, a)
+        if a < 0:
+            break
+        b = a + len(tag2)
+        c = text.find('"', b)
+        if c < 0:
+            break
+        if text[b:c]:
+            state[text[b:c]] = "active"
+        a = c
+

attachment.snippet:
293	2. **Prefer `gh` CLI** - when performing git operations (PRs, issues, checkout, etc.), prefer the GitHub CLI (`gh`) over raw `git` commands where possible
294	3. **Shared command and skill source** - keep command definitions in `.agents/commands/` and skill definitions in `.agents/skills/`. `.claude/commands` and `.cursor/commands` should be symlinks to `../.agents/commands`; `.claude/skills` should be a symlink to `../.agents/skills`. (`packages/chat` discovers slash commands from `.claude/commands`.) Skills aren't a cross-agent format yet, so non-Claude agents (Codex, Cursor, OpenCode) should read the relevant `.agents/skills/*/SKILL.md` file directly when its description matches the task.
295	4. **Workspace MCP config** - keep shared MCP servers in `.mcp.json`; `.cursor/mcp.json` should link to `../.mcp.json`. Codex uses `.codex/config.toml` (run with `CODEX_HOME=.codex codex ...`). OpenCode uses `opencode.json` and should mirror the same MCP set using OpenCode's `remote`/`local` schema.
296	5. **Mastra dependencies** - use the published upstream `mastracode` and `@mastra/*` packages. Do not add fork tarball overrides or custom patch steps unless explicitly requested.
297	6. **Plan & doc placement** - implementation plans go in `plans/` (cross-cutting) or `apps/<app>/plans/` (app-scoped); shipped plans move to `plans/done/`. Architecture/reference docs go in `<app>/docs/`. Never drop `*_PLAN.md` at an app root or inside `src/`.
298	7. **Always fix lint warnings before pushing** - CI fails on Biome warnings, not just errors (the lint script treats warnings as errors). Run `bun run lint:fix` after edits and verify `bun run lint` exits 0 before `git push`. Never push code that produces lint output, even auto-fixable formatting.
299	8. **Linear ticket format** - all tickets (creation, drafting, grooming) follow `.agents/skills/ticket-format/SKILL.md`. Read that file before creating or grooming a ticket.
300	9. **TanStack DB / Electric live queries are cache-first** - `useLiveQuery` can return persisted rows in `data` while the collection is still not `isReady`. Always render existing rows first. Use `isReady` only to decide what to show when no row/data exists yet: no data + not ready = loading/skeleton/null; no data + ready = empty/not-found. Never hide, blank, or replace existing `data` just because `isReady` is false or `isLoading` is true. This cache-first rendering rule does not apply to write/seeding side effects: wait for strict readiness before deriving missing rows or writing defaults, unless the write is provably idempotent.
301	10. **PR titles are conventional commits** - PRs are squash-merged using the PR title as the commit subject, so every title needs a conventional-commit type and scope, e.g. `feat(desktop): add copy-logs button to failed CI checks` or `fix(host-service): guard against missing PR`.

input.content:
---
name: project-teammate-idle-dot-fix
description: TEAMMATE-IDLE stuck-yellow root cause + fix — harness reports finished teammates as running forever; transcript idle_notification ledger is the deterministic truth
metadata:
  type: project
---

Stuck-yellow dots (2026-07-10, shipped 13437b4a7 `(TEAMMATE-IDLE)`): Claude Code reports finished TEAMMATES in Stop payload `background_tasks[]` as `status:"running"` FOREVER (no distinguishing field, no hooks fire for their t-prefixed ids, no on-disk record — team config.json has no member status). The [[project_dots_open_tabs_and_subagent_hold]] (BG-STALE) 900s reap only runs INSIDE hook events, so a session whose LAST event landed while `.bgactive` was <900s fresh (live case: 870s) latched yellow forever — 9 terminals observed stuck.

**Why:** the deterministic truth lives in the LEAD transcript: every finishing teammate delivers an `idle_notification` `<teammate-message>` (verified: 44/44 idle in the stuck session at its final Stop). But NOT everything is knowable — 2 teammates in session 68bced18 spawned and never wrote any transcript trace (silent death): only an idle-age rule can decide those, and user chose L1-only (no timer sweep), so silent-death windows still stick until the next prompt.

**How to apply:** superset-notify.py `_teammates_all_idle` keeps an incremental ledger (`<terminalId>.teamstate.json`, offset+state keyed by transcript path; string-scan parser — NO regex, TS template literal swallows backslashes). Drops teammate-type entries at Stop/SubagentStop before split/snapshot/decision when all tracked teammates idle; raw fork ids ("a"+≥12hex) excluded; `bg_ids` stays RAW (keep-set). Diagnosis flow that worked: dot-decisions.log → agent-notify-hook.log `bg-tasks-debug` (payload shapes) → lead transcript ledger analysis (tmp/teamstate.py). Codex xhigh notes: DOT-PERSIST rehydrates stale yellow on Ctrl+R (unfixed, needs sweep/reconcile); default codex model alias is `gpt-5.6-sol` (`-m gpt-5.6` rejected on ChatGPT account). Replay-test recipe: extract template via node eval → py_compile → feed fabricated Stop payload with SUPERSET_TERMINAL_ID=term-TEST-*, read dot-decisions.log.

<system-reminder>This memory is 9 days old. Memories are point-in-time observations, not live state — claims about code behavior or file:line citations may be outdated. Verify against current code before asserting as fact.</system-reminder>
1	# Memory Index
2	
3	- [Nightly build dependency drift + candidate-branch deadlock](project_nightly_build_dependency_drift.md) — why nightlies "never built green", TWO linked causes: (1) LOCK-REGEN — fork-only root mastra `overrides`/downgrade → electron-builder "production dependency not found version=X" 25min in; fixed 7f4902b09 (align mastra to upstream + drop override + override-consistency gate scripts/check-override-consistency.mjs + lock regen). (2) CANDIDATE-IDEMPOTENT-PUSH — the pre-LOCK-REGEN failed build orphaned candidate `nightly/desktop-v<ver>` (advance deletes it only on SUCCESS); every re-merge → new divergent SHA → plain `git push` rejected non-fast-forward → deadlock that MASKED the LOCK-REGEN fix; fixed 5ae50bf79 (delete-before-push, self-heals). FIRST green nightly 2026-06-30 (run 28441715377: merge→build→advance, desktop-v1.12.6 published). Traps: bun.lock mirrors overrides (must regen), gh defaults to upstream remote (pass --repo), Bash tool can't do PowerShell here-strings (use git commit -F), MSYS mangles `git show ref:path` colon (use MSYS_NO_PATHCONV=1)
4	- [Auto-resume on API failure](project_auto_resume_feature.md) — AUTO-RESUME feature shipped to main 2026-06-26; locked UX (away=per-terminal interaction, 60s→×3 max5, fire-at-reset+30s, default-on, Claude-only v1) + the hard traps: commandRunning is the SHELL's not the agent's (finality gate is the idle proof), 24h budget runs from first SEND not arm, xterm onData fires on TUI query replies, TerminalAgentStore binding populates late on Windows (not_found/agent_mismatch = transient)
5	- [Recycle Bin (soft-delete) feature](project_recycle_bin_feature.md) — shipped desktop-v1.12.5 (f1cd49c09); every delete entry point routes to soft-delete (deletedAt flag), permanent only in-bin, never auto-purge, 30d renderer-only DISPLAY filter; the "all delete entry points" invariant + the entry-point list Codex/grep found; deferred /simplify work (emptyRecycleBin destroyWorkspaceOnce extraction, kanban partition pushdown)

content.text:
Shipped. Summary:

**Root cause (your instinct was right — the data was there):** Claude Code reports finished teammates in the Stop payload as `status:"running"` forever, but the lead transcript records an `idle_notification` from every teammate that finishes.
